rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO MARIN COUNTY** CONS. OF DONALD PAUL LERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/20/20. Bond must be increased before an account can be approved. [CRC 7.204(a).] Notice may not be mailed before the pleading requiring the notice is filed. [LRSF 14.21 A.] It does not appear that a post-move notice of the conservatee's address has been filed; the petition at paragraph 45 shows that he moved to 56 McAllister Street, San Francisco, CA, in 2010 (although the post-move notice showed the address as 1856 McAllister Street), but paragraph 61 shows the conservatee's address in San Rafael. The (original) bank statement(s) filed on 9/3/20 has not yet been delivered to the Examiner.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHN C COX TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): 1) Conservator's authority to withdraw minimum required distributions from conservatee's IRA accounts; 2) Conservator's authority to transfer IRA distributions to conservatee's trust; 3) Allocation of gifts to principal/income. If allocated to income, Trustee's authority to withhold net income; and 4) Fees/costs of Trustee's attorneys incurred to correct order on 2nd account. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE AKIRA NAGANO REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST, DTD 11/22/05, TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. ISSUES: 1) Petitioner originally requested 1% guideline fees or $30,127.40 in trustee compensation. After submitting requested timesheets Petitioner now requests $27,114.66 in fees. The trustee timesheets show $19,633.00 earned in compensation. 2) Examiner questioned why over $250,000.00 cash is in a single account when the FDIC insurance limit is $250,000.00. Petitioner alleges, "Petitioner was surprised to get a hearing date two and a mail months after filing her petition. In hindsight had she know it would take this long to have the matter heard she would have spread the funds into 6 different savings accounts."

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDDIE COTTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/20. The petition does not show the (correct) representative capacity of the petitioner who is an administrator. [CRC 2.111 and LRSF 14.17 A.] The first account for a decedent's estate must begin with the date of death. [LRSF 14.59.] The account presumably is a request for its approval, although there is no "petition" or prayer requesting relief. [Probate Code Section 1064(b).] The account is also incomplete: there is no schedule showing the assets on hand at the end of the accounting period. [Probate Code Section 1061(a)(10).] There is also no schedule showing the fair market value of assets on hand. [Prob. C. Sect. 1063(a).] The detailed schedules supporting the summary of account must show the nature or purpose of each receipt or disbursement, the source of the receipt or name of the payee, and the date thereof. [Prob. C. Sect. 1062.] It is unclear what the "Administrative Trust Checking" account represents following the summary of account. The only asset shown on the I&A was the real property. Original bank statements are required to be filed to verify the cash balance on hand at the end of the accounting period. [LRSF 14.62.] A declaration must also be filed re the retention of original documents that includes all the allegations required by LRSF 2.11 M.4. The proof of service (Civil) is the incorrect form; the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing is required for a decedent's estate hearing, and the petitioner cannot serve notice, as she is an interested party. Notice may not be mailed before the pleading requiring notice is filed. Additional note: Decedent's son Michael A. Cotton is reportedly deceased, but the initial Petition for Letters of Administration did not set forth his date of death and did not affirmatively state whether or not he was survived by issue. [LRSF 14.26 D.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SEAN SHARMA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioners, two LLCs, request appointment as co-administrators. Court to determine whether LLCs can be appointed fiduciaries and whether they are able to be bonded as fiduciaries. 2) Item 5a (who Decedent was survived by) is incomplete and Item 6 (which indicates that Decedent was survived by next of kin) appears to be incorrect as Decedent's siblings are listed. Petitioners state that they have no knowledge regarding Decedent's family or his personal affairs other than what is set forth in the amended petition and declarations. 3) No declination to serve has been filed by those with priority. Petitioners allege that they attempted to contact them on several occasions but have been unsuccessful to date. 4) Petitioners request no bond and indicate that assets are unknown. Attachment 2 of the petition indicates that they have located $200 in a bank account and stock issued by a corporation that Decedent owned, the value of which is uncertain. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 6th amended general order.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/13/20 at Counsel's request. Petition is also incomplete at item 5 and item 7 was completed when issue reportedly survived.
